message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): URL trailing-slash preservation contract (tick 329)

Added `distributed_http_client_preserves_trailing_slash_in_path` —
pins that `DistributedHttpClient::get` transmits a URL path
byte-for-byte, preserving a trailing slash.

Why this matters: servers commonly treat `/api/events` and
`/api/events/` as **different** routes — one often returns a
collection listing, the other a specific resource or listing of
sub-resources. A client that silently stripped or appended a trailing
slash would misroute requests in a way that's invisible to the
Host + method + query-string checks we already have (routes can
agree on those and still diverge on the final slash).

Asserts the server-received request line starts with
`GET /api/events/ HTTP/1.` — including the trailing `/` before the
space + HTTP version.

Three URL-path variants are now pinned:
- trailing slash (this tick)
- explicit path without trailing (tick 321)
- empty path → default `/` (tick 324)

Test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 21s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
